In 2022, [[Warrington Wolves]] played in their 27th consecutive season in the [[Super League]] and their 19th season at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]]. Warrington were coached by [[Daryl Powell]] and they competed in both [[Super League XXVII]] and the [[2022 Challenge Cup]].

==Season review==
On 12 February, Warrington began their Super League campaign with a 22–20 away win at [[Leeds Rhinos]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36608/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-1 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 1 |publisher=RFL |date=13 February 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}}</ref> which was followed with wins over [[Castleford Tigers]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36653/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-2 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 2 |publisher=RFL |date=20 February 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}}</ref> and [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36688/betfred-super-league-round-up |title=Betfred Super League round-up|publisher=RFL |date=28 February 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}}</ref> The run of wins ended when their first loss came against [[Catalans Dragons]] in Round 4<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36745/betfred-super-league-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=6 March 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}}</ref> and they failed to record consecutive wins for the rest of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/super-league-xxvii-2022/warrington/summary.html |title=Super League XXVII 2022: Warrington Wolves |website=Love Rugby League |access-date=15 February 2024}}</ref> Warrington were knocked out of the [[Challenge Cup]] at the end of March after losing to a [[Wakefield Trinity]] side that had also beaten them in the league in the previous match.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36891/betfred-challenge-cup-round-6-round-up |title=Betfred Challenge Cup Round 6 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=27 March 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}}</ref> After a win over [[Huddersfield Giants]] in April, Warrington went on a run of five losses before defeating [[Hull F.C.]] 4–0 in late June.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60354/betfred-super-league-preview-%7C-round-16 |title=Betfred Super League Preview {{!}} Round 16 |publisher=RFL |date=23 June 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60378/betfred-super-league-round-16-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 16 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=26 June 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}}</ref> A 32–18 victory over Toulouse in mid-August helped Warrington to maintain their gap over the bottom of the table team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60725/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-thursday |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Thursday |publisher=RFL |date=11 August 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}}</ref> Warrington finished their season with 32–14 win over [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60859/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-27 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 27 |publisher=RFL |date=3 September 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}}</ref>
